Scribe: OB-Xd VST3 bridge integration session complete

- Orchestration logs: Spike (architecture), Jet (deployment), Faye (host diagnostics), Ed (verification), Coordinator (integration)
- Session log: Final bridge message-pump refactor validated
- Decision inbox merged: 6 entries covering IPlugFrame fix, versioned deployment, thread affinity root cause
- Cross-agent history updated: Team learnings documented
- Archive check: decisions.md at 152KB, entries all recent (no archiving needed)

Architectural invariant locked:
> The bridge main thread is the UI/STA thread. All window creation, COM operations, plugin loads, and IPlugView calls happen on this thread. Pipe reader is background thread only.

Validation complete:
✅ Shipped Debug bridge byte-matches rebuilt Release bridge
✅ load_ack succeeds; openEditor successfully calls attached() without deadlock
✅ OB-Xd editor incomplete UI confirmed as plugin-side (beyond minimal host scope)
✅ Versioned deployment + manifest working end-to-end
⚠️ Test coverage gap flagged: 0 automated tests currently cover VST3/UI behavior

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add Node.js/Express backend for InfraWatch MVP

- Express API server with CORS, JSON parsing, and rate limiting (120 req/min)

- GET /api/metrics/uptime for per-device uptime percentage
- HTTP polling monitor (checkDevice, startMonitoring, stopMonitoring)

commit.message:
feat: add `export-audit` CLI command for wallet transaction CSV export (#7700)

Wallet needs to export transactions as a CSV for auditing, with optional
date range filtering and fiat conversion.

## Changes

### `cli.rs`
- New `ExportAuditArgs` struct with: `--output-file`, `--start-date`,
`--end-date`, `--conversion-rate`, `--currency` (default: `USD`)
- New `ExportAudit(ExportAuditArgs)` variant in `CliCommands`
- `parse_utc_datetime` helper accepting RFC3339 and `YYYY-MM-DD` /
`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` formats

### `commands.rs`
- `ExportAudit` handler: fetches all completed transactions, filters by
date range, calls `write_audit_to_csv_file`
- `write_audit_to_csv_file`: emits CSV with columns matching the spec —
transaction type is `Deposit` (inbound/coinbase) or `Withdraw`
(outbound); transaction hash derived from first kernel hash; amounts in
XTM (Minotari); optional fiat column using supplied rate

### `wallet_modes.rs`
- Added `ExportAudit` arm to the exhaustive command-match test

## Example

```bash
minotari_console_wallet export-audit \
  --output-file audit.csv \
  --start-date 2021-01-01 \
  --end-date 2021-12-31 \
  --conversion-rate 3130.0 \
  --currency USD
```

Output CSV columns:
```
ID, Transaction Hash, Status, Transaction Type, DateTime (UTC),
From Address, To Address, Amount, AmountTicker, Amount (USD),
Txn Fee, FeeTicker, Fee (USD)
```

commit.message:
Auto-create sample config file on first run

The config file at ~/.ai-incident-investigator/config.yaml is now
automatically created with sample values when the tool runs for the
first time, so users can simply edit it instead of creating it manually.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add YAML config file support for credentials

Credentials (GitHub token, Airflow URL/user/password) can now be
configured via ~/.ai-incident-investigator/config.yaml instead of
environment variables. Resolution order: CLI args > env vars > config file.

- Add src/config.py: YAML config loader with caching
- Update AirflowClient and GitHubRepoManager to use config loader
- Add --init-config CLI flag to bootstrap the config file
- Update README with config file documentation

commit.message:
Add iTipFooty.com.au auto-tipping integration

Automates submitting prediction engine picks to iTipFooty:

- Login via POST to services/login.php with session cookie management
- Parse tipping page HTML to extract game numbers, team names, form fields
- Map prediction engine picks (team IDs) to iTipFooty H/A selections
- Submit tips via POST to services/SubmitTips.php
- Handle locked games (already kicked off) gracefully

Backend:
- New service: backend/src/services/itipfooty.ts
- New route: /api/itipfooty/status and /api/itipfooty/submit
- Credentials loaded from .env (ITIPFOOTY_USERNAME, PASSWORD, COMP_ID)
- dotenv added for env var loading
- Submission logged to DataSourceLog

Frontend:
- Settings page: iTipFooty Integration card with status display
- Submit Tips button with detailed result feedback per game
- Shows configuration instructions when not configured

Security:
- Credentials stored in backend/.env (gitignored)
- .env.example provided with empty placeholders
- Status endpoint masks username with ***

fix: Use AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var instead of hardcoded claude (#3100)

* fix: use AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var instead of hardcoded claude

The recipe runner and delegation scripts hardcoded 'claude' as the agent
binary, causing failures when amplihack is run with other agents (copilot,
codex, etc.).

Changes:
- cli.py: Set AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var at dispatch for all 5 agent cmds
- rust_runner.py: Forward --agent-binary flag to Rust recipe runner
- platform_cli.py: Read env var instead of hardcoding 'claude'
- delegate_response.py (3 mirrors): Read env var + add missing 'import os'

The Rust recipe runner companion changes are in
rysweet/amplihack-recipe-runner#29.

Fixes #3099

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review findings - empty string edge case and missed hardcoded files

- Fix orchestrator.py: use ${AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY:-claude} bash fallback
- Fix executor.py (2 locations): same bash fallback pattern for remote execution
- Fix empty string edge case: use 'or "claude"' pattern instead of default param
  in platform_cli.py and all 3 delegate_response.py mirrors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Add services/realtime, packages/ui, and apps/web

  Redis, and JWT authentication. Supports channel subscriptions, typing
  indicators, and domain event fan-out.

  Card, Badge, Spinner, Alert, Modal, Avatar, Sidebar, and DataTable
  components.

  and messages pages.

commit.message:
Phase 4 complete: full thesis first draft (5 chapters, ~12,000 words)

thesis/chapter1-introduction.md (~3,000 words):
- Motivation, research questions, chaos engineering background
- Kubernetes security model (PSA, NetworkPolicy, Falco)
- Literature review: Chaos Mesh, kube-bench, Gremlin, Trivy, gap analysis
- Ethical considerations

thesis/chapter2-methodology.md (~2,000 words):
- Design science research approach
- 5 Whys RCA motivating requirements
- MoSCoW requirements (Must/Should/Could/Won't)
- Technology selection rationale (Go, Kind, Calico, Falco, Helm)
- Experimental design (golden vs misconfigured cluster)
- Validity threats (internal, external, construct)

thesis/chapter3-architecture.md (~3,500 words):
- Full system architecture diagram
- All 5 internal packages documented
- PSA admission edge case implementation
- AlertStore thread-safety and ClearRule design
- MTTD calculation rationale
- Falco Helm chart compatibility issues and fixes
- 27-test testing strategy

thesis/chapter4-results.md (~2,500 words):
- RQ1: 100% detection, two control layers validated
- RQ2: ~4,200x faster than manual, reproducible
- RQ3: MTTD architecture sound; null in local runs (deployment constraint)
- Threats to validity: network-egress Kind limitation, sample size

thesis/chapter5-conclusion.md (~1,000 words):
- Contributions summary
- Limitations
- Future work: expanded experiments, cloud SIEM, parallel execution
- Reflection on chaos engineering applied to security

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
